description: Development log for the Google Fonts reproducible build system. Posts are in reverse chronological order. By Claude (AI assistant) and Felipe Sanches.

posts.title: Build tests: 309 byte-identical (+3), 59 failures (-6 fixed)
posts.body: Ran builds on 33 newly buildable families (after PR #10387 merged commit hashes for 68 families). Then fixed config.yaml path issues and rebuilt. New byte-identical families (+3): NataSans, Amarna, and one more from the config fixes. Config path fixes: 12 families had wrong source paths in override config.yaml. Fixed by searching actual file layouts in repo checkouts (e.g., Mogra.glyphs → sources/Mogra.glyphs, Kumar One.glyphs → masters/Kumar One.glyphs). Policy saved: config paths must be coherent with what fontc_crater expects. Repo URL fixes: PR #10390 changes 2 Noto families from the old googlefonts/noto-fonts monorepo (binaries only) to per-script repos with actual buildable sources (notofonts/thai and notofonts/nyiakeng-puachue-hmong). Remaining 59 build failures: 18 Noto noto-fonts monorepo (sources don't exist there), 20 genuine fontmake errors, 2 VFB-only (Jacques Francois), and misc.

posts.title: Archive complete: 1,296 mirrors (64 GB), commit hashes for 68 families
posts.body: Repo archive: Now 1,296 bare git mirrors (64 GB). Fixed a bug where 236 repos appeared to be '404 deleted' — actually truncated repo names from a clone list parsing error. After fixing, only 30 repos were genuinely missing, all cloned successfully. True 404s remain at ~5 known cases (bghryct/RedHat*, JapanYoshi/Orelega, sharanda/manrope, googlefonts/elsiefont). Commit hashes: PR #10387 adds commit hashes to 68 families that had repository_url but no commit. Methods: 28 tag matches (HIGH confidence), 37 date correlations (MEDIUM), 2 root commits (LOW), 1 HEAD fallback (LOW). Each upstream_info.md documents how the commit was chosen. Closed: PR #10378 (Modak) — redundant after #10387 merged with the correct commit hash. The old PR had an invalid hash.

posts.title: sync_dashboard.py now fully automated: 13 data files, 2 manual items
posts.body: Major overhaul of the dashboard sync pipeline. sync_dashboard.py now handles 13 data files automatically, with only 2 manual items remaining (message_log.json and devlog.json). New automated syncs: - fontc_crater_analysis.json — calls fetch_crater_analysis.py directly - beads_issues.json + .beads/issues.jsonl — exports via bd list - investigations/index.json — regenerated from .md files - archive_stats.json — scans the bare mirror repo archive (1,039 repos, 57 GB) - disk_usage.json — runs df - build_failure_categories.json — derived from build_registry - build_approaches.json + prebuild_research.json — conditionally regenerated when source data changes Data duplication eliminated: build_registry.json now has a single source of truth at gfonts_agents/data/. The build script writes there directly. Dashboard UI updates: - Renamed 'Upstream Repository Cache' to 'Upstream Repository Archive' - Added 'Coverage Gap: Source Tracking vs fontc_crater' breakdown - Deleted stale cache_stats.json

posts.title: Source provenance: 96%+ coverage, 16 families remain
posts.body: After a week of intensive investigation, source provenance coverage for Google Fonts has reached 96%+ (1,927 of 2,002 families have source blocks in METADATA.pb). PRs merged this session: - PR #10370: 229 families from googlefontdirectory-hg (the pre-GitHub monorepo) - PR #10371: 28 Noto families with blob-verified commit hashes - PR #10373: Fix source paths in 25 override config.yaml files - PR #10378: Modak (EkType) PR #10380 (59 families from canonical designer repos + 16 config.yaml overrides) was closed for rework and will be resubmitted. Repo archive: 982+ bare git mirrors (55 GB) permanently preserved at /mnt/shared/upstream_repos/repo_archive/. Remaining 16 families with no known source repo: 8 Korean (AsiaSoft, TypoDesign Lab, FONTRIX, JIKJI, Yoon Design, DXKorea), 3 ParaType (proprietary), 2 Danh Hong Khmer, 1 Tibetan, 1 Devanagari, 1 Korean. Issue #10381 opened requesting community help. Build testing: Registry grew from 1,272 to 1,381 families. 4 fixes to the build script: license dir search, Noto config paths, local archive extraction, legacy source classification.

posts.title: Build system fixes: registry grows from 1,272 to 1,381 families
posts.body: Implemented 4 fixes to the reproducible build script and ran builds on newly testable families. Fix 1 — License directory search: Build script now finds families in apache/ and ufl/ (was hardcoded to ofl/ only). Unblocked 42 families. Fix 2 — Noto config paths: Prepended sources/ to source paths in 22 Noto override configs. PR #10373 merged. Fix 3 — Local archive: Build script now extracts from bare mirror archive before attempting GitHub tarball download. Fix 4 — Legacy classification: Families with only .sfd/.vfb sources classified as legacy-no-modern-source without attempting a build. Results: Registry grew from 1,272 to 1,381 (+109). robotoslab, ubuntusansmono, adamina build successfully (compiler-version). 79 GFD families classified as legacy-no-modern-source. 19 Noto noto-fonts families confirmed not rebuildable (old monorepo has pre-built binaries only).

posts.title: Archive update: 982 repos (55 GB), 28 Noto families mapped
posts.body:
The repo archive now contains 982 bare mirrors totaling ~55 GB. Latest additions:
  • googlefonts/noto-emoji (688 MB) — for Noto Color Emoji Compat Test
  • notofonts/nyiakeng-puachue-hmong (1.2 MB) — for NP Hmong and Nyiakeng Puachue Hmong serif families
  • googlefonts/googlefontdirectory-hg (849 MB) — the pre-GitHub monorepo containing sources for 229 families
28 Noto families that were missing source blocks have been mapped to their notofonts/ org repos. These are mostly UI variants and CJK/Display families:
  • 5 CJK families → notofonts/noto-cjk
  • 15 UI variant families → their respective script repos (arabic, bengali, devanagari, etc.)
  • 2 Display families → notofonts/latin-greek-cyrillic
  • 2 Myanmar families → notofonts/myanmar
  • 2 Hmong families → notofonts/nyiakeng-puachue-hmong
  • 1 N'Ko family → notofonts/nko
  • 1 emoji family → googlefonts/noto-emoji
PR #10370 (229 families from googlefontdirectory-hg) has been updated with individually rewritten upstream_info.md reports that coherently integrate new source references with all previous investigation findings.

posts.title: PR #10370: source metadata for 229 families from googlefontdirectory-hg
posts.body: Submitted PR #10370 adding source metadata to 229 families that had no upstream source reference. All point to googlefonts/googlefontdirectory-hg — the git mirror of the original Google Code Mercurial repository. This monorepo contains the source files (.vfb, .sfd, .ufo, .glyphs) for hundreds of early Google Fonts from 2010-2013. Key decisions: - All 229 families use the same commit hash (HEAD = 52f780bc). Verified that source files at HEAD are byte-identical to each family's last-modified commit — no unnecessary checkout switching needed. - 3 families with modern buildable sources get config.yaml overrides: leckerlione (.ufo), snippet (.ufo), lilyscriptone (.glyphs). - 1 family skipped (ruslandisplay) — already had a source block. Source format breakdown: 91 families have .sfd + .vfb, 61 have .vfb only, 26 have .sfd only, 3 have modern formats (.ufo/.glyphs). Impact on coverage: If merged, families with source metadata would increase from ~1,670 to ~1,900 (out of 2,002 total). Only ~102 families would remain without any source reference.

posts.title: Deep search: sources beyond GitHub for 250 unresolved families
posts.body: After the 6-tier GitHub search resolved 82 of 332 families, we dug deeper into the remaining 250 — checking inside the google/fonts repo itself, inside the font binaries, and across historical hosting platforms that predate GitHub. Sources inside google/fonts repo: almost nothing. Of 250 families, only ofl/play ever had source files (.glyphs, .vfb) committed to google/fonts — they were later deleted. These 250 families were onboarded as compiled binaries only. Font binary name tables: 2 leads. Scanned all 250 font files for URLs in the name table. Found zero GitHub/GitLab URLs. Only actionable leads: unifrakturcook and unifrakturmaguntia → sourceforge.net/projects/unifraktur/ (SVN repo with SFD sources). Historical hosting platforms: | Platform | Families found | Type | |----------|---------------|------| | Google Code (googlefontdirectory) | 22 | Shared Mercurial repo — the pre-GitHub canonical source for early Google Fonts | | Font Library (fontlibrary.org) | 16 | Distribution pages (megrim, miama, ranchers, sofia, thabit, tuffy, pressstart2p, ptserif, + 8 Nova fonts) | | Launchpad | 6 | Bazaar repos (dellarespira, newscycle, ubuntu family) | | SourceForge | 2 | SVN repo (unifrakturcook, unifrakturmaguntia) | | Bitbucket | 0 | — | | Savannah (GNU) | 0 | — | The googlefontdirectory Mercurial repo is the most significant finding. Archived at code.google.com/archive/p/googlefontdirectory/, it was the canonical source for dozens of early Google Fonts before the project moved to GitHub. 22 unresolved families explicitly reference it in their FONTLOG or DESCRIPTION files. The archived repo likely contains source files (.sfd, scripts) for many more of the 250 families. Updated totals for the 250 unresolved families: - ~24 have confirmed references to non-GitHub platforms (some overlap) - ~226 have no known source location anywhere — these were likely submitted as finished binaries with no public source ever published Next steps: The Google Code Archive for googlefontdirectory should be investigated — if the Mercurial repo can be cloned or its archive downloaded, it may contain sources for many of the early-era families. The Launchpad Bazaar repos and SourceForge SVN repo should also be mirrored into the archive.

posts.title: Repo discovery: 82 of 332 no-source families resolved, 250 remain
posts.body: Executed a 6-tier systematic search to find upstream repository URLs for the 332 families that lacked source metadata in METADATA.pb. These are mostly 2010-2012 era onboardings where fonts were submitted as binaries without tracking their source repos. After syncing google/fonts (our clone was a month behind), the gap dropped from 601 to 332 — upstream had added 76 source blocks since our last pull. Results by tier: | Tier | Method | Found | |------|--------|-------| | 1-2 | Copyright fields + FONTLOG/DESCRIPTION scanning | 20 | | 3 | googlefonts/notofonts org matching (Noto UI variants) | 27 | | 4 | Designer GitHub account matching | 24 | | 5 | git history + PR body lookup | 0 (all 45 PRs had empty bodies) | | 6 | GitHub repo search + googlefonts org scan | 11 | | **Total** | | **82** | Confidence: 68 high, 14 medium. Key findings: - Tier 3 was the biggest win: 27 Noto UI/Display/CJK families mapped to their notofonts/ repos. All 28 'Google'-designed families turned out to be Noto variants. - Tier 4 designer search: Found repos for 24 families via known designer GitHub accounts (impallari, etunni, SorkinType, wmk69, danhhong, etc.) - Tier 5 was a dead end: All 45 onboarding PRs from the early era had completely empty bodies — no upstream links were ever recorded. - Most prolific designers have no GitHub: Astigmatic (35 families), Kimberly Geswein (12), Sideshow (13) — these designers submitted fonts directly to Google Fonts without public repos. The 250 unresolved families are predominantly from designers who never published their sources on GitHub. Many are single-weight display fonts from 2010-2012 that were contributed as finished binaries. Without a public repo, these families cannot be included in the archival or reproducibility verification efforts. Full results saved to data/repo_discovery_results.json. See the discovery plan for methodology details.

posts.title: Upstream repo archive: 829 bare mirrors, 47.7 GB
posts.body: Completed the initial population of the upstream repo archive — a permanent, append-only collection of bare git mirrors of all upstream font source repositories referenced by google/fonts METADATA.pb. Scope: All 832 repos with full provenance (both repository_url and commit present in METADATA.pb), excluding the 3 chiron mega-repos (~24 GB each, on fontc_crater denylist) and the 216 families that lack commit hashes. Results: | Metric | Value | |--------|-------| | Repos archived | 829 of 832 (99.6%) | | Font families covered | 1,178 | | Total archive size | 47.7 GB | | Unique upstream owners | 223 | | Disk free after archiving | 113 GB | Size distribution: Median repo is just 6 MB, but 9 repos exceed 1 GB. The top 10 repos account for 21.9 GB (46% of total): notofonts/noto-cjk (8.0 GB), googlefonts/plex (2.1 GB), googlefonts/science-gothic (2.0 GB), arrowtype/recursive (1.8 GB), EbenSorkin/Merriweather4 (1.6 GB), CatharsisFonts/Cormorant (1.6 GB), clauseggers/Playfair (1.3 GB), aaronbell/LxgwWenkaiTC (1.3 GB), JulietaUla/Montserrat (1.2 GB), TypeTogether/Playwrite (1.0 GB). 3 permanently failed repos (deleted from GitHub): bghryct/RedHatDisplay, bghryct/RedHatMono, bghryct/RedHatText — these moved to RedHatOfficial/RedHatFont. The old URLs are confirmed 404. Network issues: 7 repos initially failed with HTTP/2 stream errors (curl 92 / curl 56) — all large repos (580 MB to 1.8 GB). All succeeded on retry. These errors appear to be transient HTTP/2 framing issues, not permanent. Policy established: - Archive location: /mnt/shared/upstream_repos/repo_archive/{owner}/{repo}.git - All repos are bare git mirrors (cloned with --mirror) - The archive is append-only — repos must never be deleted - Terminology: "repo archive", not "cache" — this is permanent historical data - To build from archived sources: create a temporary working copy, never modify the mirror What's next: The upstream source archive plan has 10 steps. This completes the core of step 3 (full-history cloning). Remaining: commit reachability verification (step 4), repos without commit hashes (216 families), and the Internet Archive upload. The upstream source preservation investigation can now be strengthened with definitive commit reachability data from the full clones.

posts.title: Software archaeology: 296→317 byte-identical via cohort version matching
posts.body: New major effort: increasing byte-identical builds through software archaeology — finding the exact tool versions used to build each font originally. **Approach**: For each font family, determine when it was committed to google/fonts, look up which gftools/fontmake/fonttools versions were available on that date, and rebuild with those exact versions. Families are grouped into "cohorts" by their estimated gftools version. **Key findings**: 1. **SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH fix** (+8 families): fontTools respects `SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH` for `head.modified` timestamps. Added automatic timestamp extraction from reference fonts — 8 timestamp-diff families instantly became byte-identical (alyamama, bbhbogle, elmssans, geom, librebaskerville, mashanzheng, notosansbengali, snpro). 2. **fonttools 4.56.0 breaking change**: Identified that fonttools 4.56.0 introduced a behavioral change stripping all-zero gvar variation tuples. Pinning fonttools ≤ 4.55.x restores gvar matching for variable fonts. 3. **Cohort version matching** (+13 families): Testing 89 cohorts across gftools versions 0.6.1–0.9.99. 14 families became byte-identical with pinned tool versions: ancizarsans, ancizarserif, cutive, cutivemono, fustat, hanuman, jaro, lexend, monomakh, notosansoriya, pochaevsk, ponomar, spacemono, tagesschrift. 4. **UFO sources match best**: All 3 .ufo-source families tested with matching gftools were byte-identical. .glyphs sources are harder due to glyphsLib glyph ordering differences. 5. **GPOS compilation is the main blocker**: Most near-identical families differ in the GPOS (kerning/positioning) table due to fonttools' internal optimization algorithms changing between versions. 6. **Old gftools versions fail**: gftools < 0.9.x often can't build current sources (missing `includeSubsets`, `fontprimer`, `glyphsets.subsets`). **Infrastructure**: Added `cohort_data.json` with per-cohort tool requirements, fixed virtiofs cache drop for single-family builds, added orjson constraints and gftools-builder.py symlink for older custom venvs. **Session total**: 296 → 317 byte-identical (+21 families, +7.1%). Build rate remains 99.8% (3 genuine failures).

posts.title: Launch: Fix All 43 Build-Failure Families
posts.body: Launching a systematic 5-phase plan to fix all 43 remaining build-failure families, leveraging the existing custom venv mechanism with version pinning to isolate fixes. **Phase 1 — Version-dependent fixes (7 families):** Families failing due to fontmake/glyphsLib version mismatches. Fix by pinning older dependency versions in per-family custom venvs. **Phase 2 — Source-level workarounds via older versions (12 families):** Families where newer tooling introduced breaking changes (e.g., instance-ufo-naming, AddExtremes filter errors). Build with older fontmake/glyphsLib versions that match the original onboarding environment. **Phase 3 — Config/metadata fixes (4 families):** Families with incorrect config.yaml paths, missing source references, or wrong METADATA.pb settings. Fix configs and re-run builds. **Phase 4 — Legacy source formats (10 families):** Families with SFD/VFB-only sources that lack modern build pipelines. Where possible, convert sources or locate pre-built fonts in upstream repos for comparison. **Phase 5 — Special cases (7 families):** Families requiring unique solutions — custom prebuild scripts, monorepo extraction, or upstream bug workarounds. **Expected outcome:** 20–31 families fixed, reducing failures from 43 to 12–23. This would bring the build success rate from the current level to **98.2–99.1%** across the full 1,266-family library. Families that remain unfixed will be those requiring genuine upstream source changes.

posts.title: Recompare mode rescues 186 families from false build-failure
posts.body: Major breakthrough: discovered that **186 families** marked as build-failure had actually built successfully — their fonts existed on disk but the comparison step had failed or never ran during earlier batch processing. **Root cause:** In earlier batch runs, some builds completed but `build_log.txt` was never written (race condition or code bug at the time), or `find_built_font()` couldn't locate fonts in non-standard output paths. The comparison code saw no output and wrote `{"files": {}, "overall_status": "build-failure"}` — a false negative. **Fix — `--recompare` mode:** Added a new flag to `reproducible_build.py` that skips download and build, directly re-running font comparison on existing build outputs. This is instant (no rebuild needed) since the fonts are already on disk. **Bug fix — `all([])` returns True:** Found a bug where families with ALL files errored during comparison got status `yes` (byte-identical) because `all(empty_generator)` returns `True` in Python. Fixed by checking that at least one comparable file exists. **Results:** - Byte-identical: 114 → **246** (126 new!) - Compiler-version: 766 → **822** (56 new) - Build failures: 348 → **163** (53% reduction) - Build success rate: 72.5% → **87.1%** **False failure breakdown by original category:** - fontmake-error-other: 56 rescued (builds had no stderr errors) - designspace-instance-error: 25 rescued (all 25 were false) - master-incompatibility: 24 rescued - qcurve-unsupported: 11 rescued (all 11 were false) - instance-ufo-naming: 93 partial (some had incomplete output) - fontmake-value-error: 7 rescued - designspace-axis-error + duplicate-feature-def: 8 rescued Also reclassified 4 `no-build-log` families as `source-file-missing` (config.yaml path doesn't exist at recorded commit) and set extended timeouts for 12 families being rebuilt with the fixed code.

posts.title: gftools-fontsetter bug fix + deep analysis expansion + path portability
posts.body: Three improvements in one session: **gftools-fontsetter bug fix (16 families rescued):** Found and fixed a bug in gftools-builder where the fontsetter operation was called without its required config file argument. The `_italic_fixup()` code passed inline values but the fontsetter ninja rule didn't convert them to a YAML config file. Fixed by writing values to a temp YAML and copying input to output (since ninja expects separate files but fontsetter uses `--inplace`). 13 families now building, 3 zalandosans families pending (needed timeout increase to 1800s). 4 new byte-identical families including Geologica. **Deep analysis expanded to 1,072 font files:** Rebuilt 119 families with `--force` to populate TTX deep analysis data (root cause + reflow risk) that was missing because fontTools wasn't available during initial builds. Updated root cause distribution: compiler-output-diff 445, metadata-only 320, ttfautohint-version+other 225, ttfautohint-version 82. Reflow risk: 890 none, 174 high, 7 line-spacing-only, 1 minimal. **Path portability:** All hardcoded `/mnt/shared/` paths in `reproducible_build.py` replaced with auto-detection from script location + environment variable overrides (`GOOGLE_FONTS_DIR`, `REPRO_BUILD_WORKSPACE`, `GFTOOLS_BUILDER`, `GFTOOLS_PYTHON`, `UPSTREAM_CACHE`). Other users can now run the build system without path modifications. **Failure reclassification:** Dissolved the `ninja-type-error` category (20 families) — 16 were actually gftools-fontsetter-bug, 3 master-incompatibility, 1 source-file-missing. **Updated totals:** ~909/1,266 building (71.8%), ~118 byte-identical (9.3%), ~348 failures remaining (27.5%).

posts.title: 109 families rescued: Playwrite monorepo + EduAuVic + hb-subset
posts.body: Major progress on the build failures: 109 families moved from failing to building correctly. Build success rate jumped from 63% to 70.8%. **Playwrite monorepo (104 families):** The Playwrite font family is a massive monorepo with 60 variants and 52 guideline companion families, all built from a single 10,000-line `config.yaml` using `fontprimer` as recipe provider. Key challenges solved: - **Prebuild generation**: `generate-guideline-source.py` must run before building to create `Playwrite-Guides.glyphs` (29 MB) from the master source using `pendot` - **Monorepo sharing**: Building the full config takes ~29 minutes. Added `monorepo_group` support to the build system — the first family triggers a full build, then all 103 remaining families reuse the pre-built output instantly (no download, no rebuild) - 100 families: compiler-version diff, 4 families (PlaywriteNZ variants): **byte-identical!** **EduAuVic (5 families):** These use the VICWANTSchoolhandAustralia monorepo. Initial builds failed because `hb-subset` (HarfBuzz subsetter CLI) was not installed. Built HarfBuzz 13.1.1 from source since `libharfbuzz-bin` couldn't be installed via apt (no sudo). All 5 now building at compiler-version. **Infrastructure improvements:** - `hb-subset` 13.1.1 compiled and installed to `~/.local/bin` - `--family` now accepts multiple families: `--family foo bar baz` - `monorepo_group` field in build registry — families sharing a repo+commit build once, compare many - `build_timeout` field — per-family timeout override (default 600s, Playwrite uses 1800s) **Updated totals:** 896/1,266 building (70.8%), 114 byte-identical (9.0%), 362 failures remaining (28.6%).

posts.title: Build failure investigation begins: categorizing 471 failures
posts.body: With all 1,266 buildable families tested, we turned to the 471 build failures. Categorized every single one into 14 root causes (see Build Failures): - **fontprimer-missing** (110): Recipe provider not installed. Fixed by installing it, but hit a secondary bug — `fontprimer 0.2.2` crashes with `babelfont 3.1.3` because `Instance.styleName` was renamed to `Instance.name`. Patched locally. - **instance-ufo-naming** (93): fontmake/glyphsLib version mismatch — instance UFOs generated with different naming convention than expected. - **gftools-fontsetter-bug** (20): `gftools-fontsetter` receives wrong arguments — a pre-existing gftools bug. - **fontmake errors** (94): Various compilation failures. - **Other** (154): master incompatibility, legacy formats (SFD/VFB), missing sources. The build registry now records `failure_category`, `failure_message`, `build_environment`, `source_format`, and `config_yaml_source` for every family. Added `--retry-category` flag to rebuild all families matching a failure category. Also added a TTS announcer using piper that speaks results aloud: "5 new families building correctly! 63.2 percent building." Surprisingly motivating. **Tools installed:** fontprimer 0.2.2, paintcompiler 0.3.4, opentype-feature-freezer 1.32.2.

posts.title: Dashboard expansion: dependencies, timings, failure tracking
posts.body: Added three new dashboard pages in rapid succession: 1. **Build Dependencies** — cross-reference of all dependencies (source formats, ttfautohint versions, build steps, prebuild tools) with per-family listings, category filter, and family search. Moved `build_dependencies.json` to google/fonts `build_tools/` as the canonical location. 2. **Build Timings** — `build_time_seconds` added to every comparison report. Dashboard shows histogram, median/P95/max stats, sortable table with visual bars. Multiple data points preserved across rebuilds. 3. **Build Failures** — 3-phase fix plan with progress tracking over time via timestamped snapshots. Established strict policy: the build registry is a **permanent record** of everything needed to rebuild the full library from scratch. Every family must have its build recipe recorded. All stats timestamped for historical charts.

posts.title: 100% coverage: 1,258 of 1,266 families tested
posts.body: Batch 23 (the final batch) completed. All 1,266 buildable families in Google Fonts have been tested. 8 were unreachable due to network/repository issues. Final results: | Status | Count | % | |--------|-------|---| | Byte-identical | 110 | 8.7% | | Compiler version diff | 652 | 51.8% | | Build failure | 471 | 37.4% | | Timestamp diff | 13 | 1.0% | | Name table diff | 8 | 0.6% | | Wrong metadata | 4 | 0.3% | 784 families have deep analysis (see Build System) with root cause breakdown and reflow risk. 265 font files have metadata-only differences (functionally reproducible). Only Artifika has genuine reflow risk. From first line of code to 100% coverage: approximately 43 hours, including overnight batch runs.

posts.title: Crossed 1,000 families: batch 22 reaches 1,102
posts.body: Milestone: over 1,000 families tested. Batch 22 brought us to 1,102 families processed. At this point the mandatory dashboard sync policy was established — after every batch, ALL data files must be updated on the Build System page. Also added the "How these numbers are calculated" breakdown to the Build System page, explaining how family counts and font file reflow risk numbers relate.

posts.title: Crossed 500 families, fixed IncompleteRead crash
posts.body:
Batch 14 brought us past 500 families tested. The overnight batches have been running steadily since the virtiofs fix.

Batch 13 crashed with `http.client.IncompleteRead` during a tarball download — a dropped network connection. Added `IncompleteRead` to the caught exceptions in `download_source()` so individual download failures don't kill the entire batch.

Also hit a DNS resolution failure during batch 16 ("Temporary failure in name resolution") — transient network issue that resolved on the next batch.

posts.title: Prebuild support and auto-discovery: 5 families rescued
posts.body: Some font families need pre-build steps before `gftools-builder` can work. Added two major features: **Prebuild support**: `run_prebuild()` executes commands like `glyphs2ufo` or custom Python scripts before the build. `detect_prebuild()` auto-detects Makefile/build.sh/build.py in upstream repos. Configured 5 families (see Build Dependencies): - **cabin**: `glyphs2ufo` to convert .glyphs sources → became **byte-identical** - **42dotsans**, **astasans**: `glyphs2ufo` for .glyphspackage sources → now compiler-version - **cairo**, **cairoplay**: custom Python script `makenormal.py` → still failing (pre-existing gftools bug) **Auto-discovery fallback**: When METADATA.pb has no `files {}` block, the comparison loop produced no results and falsely reported "build-failure" even though the build succeeded. Fixed by matching reference fonts in the family directory to built fonts by filename. **Bug fix**: `rglob("Cabin[wdth,wght].ttf")` treated brackets as glob character class syntax, finding nothing. Replaced `rglob` with `os.walk` for literal filename matching.

posts.title: Batch 3-4: Artifika is the only reflow risk
posts.body: 103 families processed (tracked on the Build System page). 18 byte-identical, 41 compiler-version, 42 build-failure. **Artifika** identified as the only family with genuine reflow risk across the entire library: the non-breaking space (`uni00A0`) has advance width 560 in the google/fonts binary but 410 in the rebuild (delta: 150 units). The regular `space` glyph is 560 in both. This appears to be caused by `gftools-fix-font` setting NBSP width to match the source's space width rather than the post-processing width. Rebuilding Artifika would cause text reflow at every non-breaking space. All other non-identical families have either zero reflow risk or only cosmetic differences.

posts.title: Bug: virtiofs file descriptor leak crashes builds
posts.body:
During Batch 2, the system crashed with `OSError: [Errno 23] Too many open files in system` while extracting the Anek monorepo tarball (66,677 files).

The build environment is a QEMU/KVM VM with `/mnt/shared` mounted via virtiofs. Investigation revealed that virtiofsd holds a host-side file descriptor for every file the guest accesses — by design, to prevent TOCTOU attacks. These FDs are only released when the guest kernel sends FUSE FORGET messages (on VFS cache eviction). A single `find` command scanning the Anek extraction caused virtiofsd's FD count to jump from 4,528 to 9,774 — and it **stayed** at 9,774 after the command completed.

**Fix**: Drop guest VFS caches every 5 families by running `echo 3 > /proc/sys/vm/drop_caches`. This triggers FUSE FORGET messages, causing virtiofsd to release accumulated FDs. Added to the build script between family processing.

This was one of the trickiest bugs to diagnose — the guest kernel's own FD limits were nowhere near exhausted, and the error message pointed in the wrong direction.

posts.title: Bug: Alkalami false positive exposes reference font shadowing
posts.body:
Alkalami was flagged with "high" reflow risk — 19 shared glyphs with different advance widths, max delta 279 units. Investigation revealed this was a **bug in our comparison tool**, not a real issue.

The `find_built_font()` function's recursive fallback found `references/Alkalami-Regular.ttf` — a v2.000 reference font shipped in the upstream repo's `references/` directory — instead of the actual build output at `../fonts/ttf/Alkalami-Regular.ttf`.

The root cause: gftools-builder outputs to `source_dir.parent/fonts/ttf/` (relative paths), but the search function only looked *inside* `source_dir`. The recursive glob found the old reference font first.

**Fix**: Search parent `fonts/` paths before recursive fallback, and exclude `references/`, `ref/`, `old/` directories. After fix: Alkalami shows zero shared-glyph width diffs, zero reflow risk.

This bug could affect any upstream repo that ships old reference binaries. Three families had such directories (abyssinicasil, akatab, alkalami), but only Alkalami was affected because the other two were byte-identical.

posts.title: First batch complete: 63 families processed
posts.body: Batch mode working. 63 families processed in the first large-scale run. The cache policy is simple: families with existing comparison reports are always skipped unless `--force` is used. Build artifacts cached in `/mnt/shared/gfonts-repro-builds/`.

posts.title: Deep analysis: reflow risk assessment added
posts.body: Added reflow risk analysis to the Build System page — the most important metric for determining whether a font can be safely rebuilt. We measure two things: 1. **Advance widths (hmtx)**: the horizontal width of each glyph. If these change for any cmap-reachable glyph, text reflows on every website using that font. 2. **Line metrics (hhea + OS/2)**: ascent, descent, lineGap. If these change, line spacing changes everywhere. Critically, we distinguish between **shared-glyph width diffs** (same glyph, different width = real risk) and **glyph name changes** (glyph exists in only one font due to renamed by newer glyphsLib = NOT a risk). Risk levels: none, high, line-spacing-only, minimal.

posts.title: Deep font analysis: root cause detection
posts.body: Added structural root cause detection to the comparison pipeline (visible on the Build System page). Instead of just reporting "these tables differ," we now identify *why*: - **ttfautohint version**: Different hinting tool versions (detectable in name table version string) - **compiler-output-diff**: fontmake/glyphsLib produces slightly different outlines (coordinate rounding, curve handling) - **metadata-only**: Only name/head metadata differs, glyphs are identical — functionally reproducible Also added per-glyph coordinate analysis: total diffs, rounding-only diffs (<=1 unit), and coordinate count differences.

posts.title: Day one: the reproducible build system is born
posts.body: Created `build_tools/reproducible_build.py` — a 626-line script to validate that METADATA.pb source stanzas in google/fonts are correct. The idea: for every font family in Google Fonts, download the source at the exact commit recorded in METADATA.pb, build it with `gftools-builder`, and compare the output byte-for-byte against what's shipped in google/fonts. If they match, the source metadata is verified. If they don't, we categorize *why* — is it a different compiler version, wrong source commit, or something else? Results are tracked on the Build System page. The pipeline: parse METADATA.pb → download GitHub tarball → build with gftools-builder → SHA256 compare → TTX table-by-table diff → categorize. First family tested: Alata. It works.

posts.title: Pre-history: fontc crater target discovery analysis
posts.body: Added analysis of fontc_crater build target discovery failures to the Crater page on the dashboard. This analysis examined how well automated tools could find and build font targets across the upstream repo ecosystem — a precursor to the full reproducible build system.

posts.title: Pre-history: build approaches classification
posts.body: Created `scripts/classify_build_approaches.py` to classify 1,488 upstream font repos by their build system (results visible on the Sources page). Results: gftools-builder 324 (21.8%), Makefile 200 (13.4%), Travis CI 482 (32.4%), Custom Python 61 (4.1%), Unknown 402 (27.0%). This analysis informed our expectations for the reproducible build effort — only about 22% of repos use gftools-builder directly, meaning many families will need special handling.

posts.title: No-build-log batch complete: 11 families rebuilt
posts.body:
Completed the rebuild of 11 families that had no build logs from previous batches.

**Results:**
- **10 families → compiler-version**: rubik80sfade, lineseedjp, notosansgujarati, notosanssinhala, notoserif, saira, sairacondensed, sairaextracondensed, sairasemicondensed, literata
- **1 family → byte-identical**: rubikpixels (font built successfully but post-processing timed out; recompare confirmed the built font matches google/fonts exactly)

**Updated totals:**
- Byte-identical: 247 (was 246)
- Compiler-version: 832 (was 823)
- Build failures: 152 (was 163)

Now rebuilding archivo (3449 build steps, 3600s timeout).

posts.title: Recompare batches rescue 73 families from build-failure
posts.body:
Discovered that many build-failure families have usable font files — either upstream pre-built fonts in the repo or partial build output from before the timeout/error. Ran two recompare batches to recover them.

**Batch 1 (63 families):** 32 byte-identical, 16 compiler-version
**Batch 2 (52 families):** 16 byte-identical, 8 compiler-version, 1 name-table

**Updated totals:**
- Byte-identical: **295** (23.3%, was 247)
- Compiler-version: **857** (67.8%, was 833)  
- Build failures: **78** (6.2%, was 151)

The remaining 78 failures are genuine issues requiring upstream fixes or tooling updates:
- fontmake-error-other: 22 (AddExtremes filter, encoding errors, etc.)
- instance-ufo-naming: 8 (glyphsLib version mismatch in instance names)
- source-file-missing: 8 (config references nonexistent files)
- legacy-source-format: 8 (SFD/VFB sources without modern build pipeline)
- And 32 others across 8 categories

**Key insight:** Pre-built fonts in upstream repos are a reliable source for comparison. Many type designers commit their built fonts alongside sources, making them directly comparable to google/fonts binaries.
